nodes.label: ScoreBoard Application
nodes.rationale: Mobile-first, Next.js score-tracking app for tabletop and card games, featuring a game stopwatch, support for up to 10 players with unique colors, persistent scores, leader calculation, and bulk score adjustments. Integrity mode: benchmark.

nodes.label: R2: Core ScoreBoard Features
nodes.rationale: Setup Screen (player add/delete/colors up to 10), In-Game Screen (leader indicator, live stopwatch, per-player increment/decrement), and a Bulk Action Bar for multi-player adjustments.

nodes.label: R3: State Persistence
nodes.rationale: Session states (players, scores, elapsed stopwatch time, active timer status) must persist locally to survive browser refreshes and page reloads.

nodes.label: R4: E2E Verification Suite
nodes.rationale: Programmatic E2E tests (Playwright or Cypress) verify core flows: game creation, player limits, stopwatch function, leader recalculation, bulk score actions, state persistence across reloads, and end-game confirmation.

nodes.label: useGameState Hook
nodes.rationale: Central hook managing screen routing ('home' | 'setup' | 'ingame'), active game session, and setup configuration draft. Located at hooks/useGameState.ts.

nodes.label: LocalStorage Persistence
nodes.rationale: Application automatically saves state to local storage under key 'scoreboard_game_state'. Next.js SSR-safeguards applied by loading storage data inside a client-side useEffect hook to prevent hydration mismatches.

nodes.label: Stopwatch Interval Pattern
nodes.rationale: To avoid resetting the interval timer on unrelated component re-renders, updates are performed using React's functional state update setActiveGame(prev => ...) with dependencies scoped strictly to activeGame?.isRunning.

nodes.label: Mobile-First Layout
nodes.rationale: Styled exclusively for a single-column phone viewport width (~390px) to deliver an optimized mobile experience. Soft off-white/lavender background (#EEEEF8), deep indigo brand (#4B45D4). Cards border-radius: 20px, pill elements border-radius: 999px.

nodes.label: Setup Validation Constraints
nodes.rationale: Maximum 10 players. Strict color uniqueness prevents two players from sharing the same color. Non-empty game title and at least one player required before START GAME button is active.

nodes.label: Leader Calculation Logic
nodes.rationale: Recalculates the highest score player in real-time. Ties award the leader badge to the first player in the list.

nodes.label: Bulk Action Bar
nodes.rationale: Slides up from the bottom when >=1 player is selected. Offers multi-player score increment/decrement.

nodes.label: End Game Confirmation Modal
nodes.rationale: Requires a confirmation modal popup to prevent accidental progress loss.

nodes.label: Screen Routing State Machine
nodes.rationale: Routes between 'home' | 'setup' | 'ingame' screens managed centrally in useGameState.

nodes.label: Opaque-Box Test Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Opaque-box, requirement-driven. No dependency on implementation design.

nodes.label: Playwright Test Runner
nodes.rationale: Playwright E2E Test runner invoked via 'npm run test:e2e' or 'npx playwright test'. Configured for Chromium browser, headless testing, auto-spins Next.js dev server on port 3000 via webServer option.

nodes.label: Tier 1: Feature Coverage
nodes.rationale: >=5 tests per feature covering all happy paths (Total: 40 tests).

nodes.label: Tier 2: Boundary & Corner Cases
nodes.rationale: Error handling, max limits, negative numbers, overflow checks (Total: 40 tests).

nodes.label: Tier 3: Cross-Feature Interactions
nodes.rationale: Pairwise coverage of major feature interactions (Total: 8 tests).

nodes.label: Tier 4: Real-World Scenarios
nodes.rationale: >=5 realistic application scenarios (Total: 5 tests). Scenarios include 2-player card game, 4-player board game with ties/shifts, bulk scoring/deselection, mid-game player addition & persistence, complete game lifecycle with stopwatch checks.

nodes.label: E2E Test Suite Ready
nodes.rationale: Command: 'npm run test:e2e' (or 'npx playwright test'). Expected: all tests pass with exit code 0 once application is fully implemented. Currently 81 pass, 16 fail pending In-Game UI, stopwatch details, and bulk action bar implementation.

nodes.label: Player Avatar Color-Variant Asset Set (12 .webp swatches: Blue, Cyan, Green, Lime, Orange, Pink, Purple, Red, Rose, Sky, Violet, Yellow)
nodes.norm_label: player avatar color-variant asset set (12 .webp swatches: blue, cyan, green, lime, orange, pink, purple, red, rose, sky, violet, yellow)
